facts.statement: The projection organizes quiescent, inflammatory, proliferative, and fibrotic samples into compact regions with density-aware direct labels rather than relying on a detached legend alone.
Each represented state retains samples from multiple cohorts, with cross-domain support fractions ranging from 58% to 71%.

design_notes: Treat embedding geometry as a representation diagnostic rather than a quantitative distance proof.
Encode biological state and source domain with separate channels so cluster identity is not confused with cohort structure.
Use sparse centroid labels and a support panel to keep dense scatter annotations readable.
